result:
## Summary

I ran a full SEO audit — live site + GSC export + source repo — for Lumina, an AI meeting assistant that appears to have pivoted from an earlier product called **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool). That pivot is the root cause of nearly everything wrong here.

### What I found and fixed in the repo

| Issue | Severity | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| **Root `<head>` metadata still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"** with an inbox-zero description and `og-inboxzap.png`, even though the page copy was already updated to Lumina | Critical | Rewrote `layout.tsx` metadata to Lumina branding, added `metadataBase`, canonical, Twitter card, and site-wide Organization JSON-LD |
| **Blog index (`/blog`) is 100% client-rendered and fetches `/api/posts`, which 404s** — every visitor and every crawler sees a permanent "Could not load posts" / spinner. This orphaned all 12 blog posts (the only pages with real historical demand — ~13k combined monthly impressions per GSC) | Critical | Rebuilt `blog/page.tsx` as a server component with the 12 real posts (title/excerpt pulled from the live pages) linked directly — no client fetch dependency |
| **`sitemap.ts` had a literal `TODO: include blog posts`** — sitemap only listed 4 URLs, so posts had no discovery path even as a backstop | High | Sitemap now enumerates all 12 posts from a shared `blog/posts.ts` module (16 URLs total) |
| **`/faq` is linked from the header nav on every page and 404s live.** `next.config.js` redirects it to `/#faq`, but the homepage had no `#faq` anchor — the "fix" was itself incomplete | High | Added a real FAQ section (with `FAQPage` JSON-LD) to the homepage at `#faq`, and pointed nav directly at `/#faq` |
| Homepage had no `SoftwareApplication` schema wired into the actual markup (schema.org note in the audit skill: verify via rendered HTML, not assumptions) | Medium | Added `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD to the homepage |

Verified with a full `next build` + local prod server: build passes, `/`, `/blog`, `/sitemap.xml` all serve correctly, all 12 post links and sitemap entries resolve.

### The bigger issue I can't fix in code — content/keyword mismatch

All 12 existing blog posts (confirmed via titles: *"How to Reach Inbox Zero," "10 Gmail Filters," "Bulk Delete Emails Guide,"* etc.) target the **old InboxZap keyword space** — exactly matching `gsc/Queries.csv`. None of it touches Lumina's actual space (AI meeting assistant, transcription, action items). So even fully fixed technically:
- There's **zero content** capturing Lumina's real demand today.
- The legacy posts, once re-crawlable, will pull in inbox-cleanup searchers with no interest in a meeting assistant — vanity impressions, not qualified traffic.

**Recommendation:** don't just delete the legacy posts (they carry real indexed history/backlink equity) — but they need a decision: add a bridge CTA to Lumina in each post, or 301 the ones with no plausible bridge, and prioritize net-new content against Lumina's actual keyword space (meeting notes, action items, Zoom/Teams/Meet transcription, comparison pages vs. Otter.ai/Fireflies). I couldn't do this in-repo since post bodies live outside the files I have access to (no `/api/posts` backend visible here).

### Two things to flag to your team, outside this repo's scope
- `/api/posts` returning 404 is a backend/CMS issue — the frontend fix above works around it, but that endpoint needs a real fix.
- `npm install` flagged a **critical security vulnerability** in `next@14.2.5` — worth a patch, unrelated to SEO but noting since I touched `package.json`'s dependency tree.
